task: d=16 BnB feasibility for Sidon C_{1a} >= 1.281
tier_structure_v4: B1 (μ-space corner LB: μ^T A_W μ >= Σ lo_i lo_j; sound w/o PSD assumption)
F (vectorized quadratic Shor/McCormick LB across all windows)
L_single (CVXPY/MOSEK LP/SDP top-K candidate windows; B43 widest-window-first)
L_joint (joint Shor across top-K windows, K=4 default)
split (gradient-weighted axis bisection; B45 caps depth=1 if d<=4, B46 skips wasteful root splits)

worst_case_cell.concern: cell radius is +/- 1/(2S) = +/- 1/128 per coord; near-uniform mu's are exactly the worst-case for tier_F/L_single since gradient is small. L_joint needs to close the 0.28-gap on a fat box.

a_speedup.what: Symmetry reduction (S_d invariance of TV_W under index reversal + palindromic conjugation)
a_speedup.why: TV_W(mu) is invariant under mu -> reverse(mu). Quotient cell count by ~factor 2 trivially; combined with sorting orbit reps by 'shells' (compositions modulo Sym), conservatively 5-10x. But this does NOT change the asymptotic — C(79,15) is still 5e14 after a 10x reduction.

b_bound_tightness.what: Use Lasserre d>=4 moment-LP (or Farkas-certified val(d=4)>=1.0963 from MEMORY) as the L_joint replacement near uniform; ratchet up to d=6 or d=8 ONLY on the small set of unkilled near-uniform cells.
b_bound_tightness.why: F/L_single/L_joint(K=4) all use Shor — degree-2 RLT. Near uniform mu*=1/16, the off-diag epsilon products are tiny but quadratic; Shor lies above degree-2 SOS hull and degrades to F itself. Lasserre d=4 (sigma_4) gives the only known tightening that converges to TV_W on the box.

c_realistic_wallclock.with_a+b_only: infeasible at d=16 S=64 — even at 1e9 cells/s on B1-fast path, 5e15 cells = 60 days *just* to enumerate, ignoring memory/IO. Hard cells alone (5-10%) at 10 cells/s = 10^4-10^5 *years* on a single node.
c_realistic_wallclock.verdict: Coarse BnB at d=16 with S=4d is NOT feasible on any horizon Claude/the user can realistically deploy. The S=128 (n=2) variant is astronomically worse (~10^20 cells).

killing_constraint: THROUGHPUT, by 5+ orders of magnitude. Bound tightness is secondary: even with an oracle that certifies every cell in 1 ns, C(79,15) = 5.39e15 evaluations is ~62 days at 1 GHz. The composition count C(S+d-1,d-1) blows up super-exponentially in d; at d=16 the BnB is fundamentally combinatorial-limited.
alternative_paths: Lower d: d=8 has C(31,7) = 2.6e6 — tractable.  But M(d=8) needs check; CLAUDE.md history suggests d=8 margins exist (Farkas val(4)>=1.0963 uses d<=8).
Adaptive non-uniform grid: only resolve near-uniform shell at fine S, use coarse S elsewhere. Saves ~10^3-10^5x but still infeasible at d=16.

Symmetry-quotient BnB: enumerate Sym(d)-orbit reps only. Reduction factor ~d!/|Aut(c)| ~ 16!/few = 10^13x. THIS could make d=16 conceivable: 5e15 / 2e13 ~ 250 orbits-per-shell, feasible. BUT requires non-trivial code rewrite + correctness proof that TV_W respects this symmetry (only partially true — the WINDOW choice depends on coord order).
bottom_line: d=16 coarse-BnB with v4 cascade is infeasible by ~6 orders of magnitude in wall-clock, regardless of bound-tightness improvements. The killing constraint is the C(S+d-1,d-1) cell count. Symmetry-quotient enumeration (orbits under reversal + appropriate permutation subgroup commuting with windowing) is the ONLY plausible route to make d=16 viable, and even that is a stretch. The 0.281 margin at the uniform cell is healthy — bound tightness is NOT the blocker.
